mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP and housing starts indicate weak aggregate demand, creating downward pressure on corporate revenues and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production signals weakening supply-side capacity, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 55%+ of instances where INDPRO declines by 2%+.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs per month indicates robust labor market demand, driving consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit availability (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) creates mixed demand signals, leading to volatile S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 5% and HOUST falls > 1.5% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS/HOUST movements in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish FEDFUNDS pivot after a tightening cycle reduces term premiums, boosting equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS peaks and CPIAUCSL declines > 0.5pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 55%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS peaks and CPIAUCSL declines.
